fields.description:
In some clusters outside of Yarn, the machines resources are not fully utilized, e.g., resource usage is quite low at night.  
To better utilize the resources while keep the existing SLA of the cluster, Lease/Reclaim Extension to Yarn is introduced.

comments.body: This is very similar work to YARN-5215 and YARN-1011 / YARN-5202.  It would be good to understand how this is fundamentally different, because at first glance it appears the desired behavior can be accomplished via one or more of those other efforts.

Hi
It will be better if you could share a more details about this work such as a document since a detailed patch is submitted.
From the patch, it looks like you are planning to introduce a LeaseManager to control various lease requests placed from cli and try to push those resource for other uses. I am not very sure, but a detailed document will really help to know about the use cases which you are trying to address. As Jason told, it looks similar to the overcommit effort happening in YARN-1011/YARN-5215.

Thank you!
I'll check the issues and update you here.

Thank you!
 I'll check the issues and update you here.
I've uploaded the document, FYI:)

Yarn-5261 main purpose is: lease the unused resource on some nodes which already having some important services (outside of Yarn), and make the lease/reclaim controllable by the nodes' owners (landlords).
It also uses zookeeper to make the lease/reclaim operation recoverable even there's RM restarting or failover, so it's very stable.

I checked the issues you mentioned, and I got:
	* Yarn-5215:
		It has similar purpose as my issue: leverage the unused resources on some nodes.
		But, it's another approach, and can get different result.

		It assumes all nodes are schedulable by Yarn, and changes the unallocated resource by reducing the external utilization on each node dynamically.  
		My issue (Yarn-5261) is different:
		1. don't assume all nodes are schedulable (though Yarn NM is running on all nodes).  The nodes are schedulable only when the landlord leases them.  So, it's more controllable.
		2. the total schedulable resource is estimated by the landlord and set when node is leased, which is steadier than changing after calculating the utilization.  And, landlord can plan how to use the resources better, not rely on automatic calculating.
		3. when the schedulable resource is over used, the containers will be killed to make sure obeying the landlord.


	* Yarn-1011:
		It's more like a Yarn scheduler enhancement for containers, but don't consider the external utilization at all.
		It's not proper for the scenario that running yarn on nodes with other services.
	
	* Yarn-5202:
		It's about resource overcommit, not the same purpose.

So, they look similar, but are different.  Users can use them based on their requirements.
Sunil, I've replied to Jason.  Could you please help check it too?:) Thank you!

[~yuuuuuuuu] thanks for the clarifications.

I understand there are slight nuances for your use case but generally it's good to consolidate related efforts as otherwise we will have an explosion of specialized features in YARN (already see a trend). IIUC, this is what [~jlowe] and [~sunilg] are both alluding to.

We do have the ability to reserve cluster resources as part of YARN-1051/YARN-2572/YARN-2573. Would that be sufficient or do you need to be able to do it at node level? If the latter is the case, you can consider achieving it in combination of reservation system with node labels (YARN-4193), intuition is each node that can be leased has a label (which can be it's name).

Another option is to have LeaseManager run outside of YARN _initially_ and use the dynamic node resource configuration (YARN-291) to affect the change you want.

Thoughts?

Thanks [~subru], 
Even i had the same in my mind either we can achieve the same by using the {{dynamic node resource configuration (YARN-291)}} or having a policy for the partitions to specify when to share the partition resources to others which i was trying to capture as part of YARN-4425,  
But for other suggestion
bq. combination of reservation system with node labels (YARN-4193), intuition is each node that can be leased has a label (which can be it's name)
IIUC they ([~yuuuuuuuu] ) are not having all the services running on top of YARN hence they want to share the nodes in off peak hours when these services will not be impacted. So i believe better solution would be to either externally control through Dynamic resource configuration or have policy on partitions to schedule during offpeak hours in NonExclusive mode (and ensure that the this partition is not configured to any queue.

comments.body: This is very similar work to YARN-5215 and YARN-1011 / YARN-5202.  It would be good to understand how this is fundamentally different, because at first glance it appears the desired behavior can be accomplished via one or more of those other efforts.

Hi
It will be better if you could share a more details about this work such as a document since a detailed patch is submitted.
From the patch, it looks like you are planning to introduce a LeaseManager to control various lease requests placed from cli and try to push those resource for other uses. I am not very sure, but a detailed document will really help to know about the use cases which you are trying to address. As Jason told, it looks similar to the overcommit effort happening in YARN-1011/YARN-5215.

Thank you!
I'll check the issues and update you here.

Thank you!
 I'll check the issues and update you here.
I've uploaded the document, FYI:)

Yarn-5261 main purpose is: lease the unused resource on some nodes which already having some important services (outside of Yarn), and make the lease/reclaim controllable by the nodes' owners (landlords).
It also uses zookeeper to make the lease/reclaim operation recoverable even there's RM restarting or failover, so it's very stable.

I checked the issues you mentioned, and I got:
	* Yarn-5215:
		It has similar purpose as my issue: leverage the unused resources on some nodes.
		But, it's another approach, and can get different result.

		It assumes all nodes are schedulable by Yarn, and changes the unallocated resource by reducing the external utilization on each node dynamically.  
		My issue (Yarn-5261) is different:
		1. don't assume all nodes are schedulable (though Yarn NM is running on all nodes).  The nodes are schedulable only when the landlord leases them.  So, it's more controllable.
		2. the total schedulable resource is estimated by the landlord and set when node is leased, which is steadier than changing after calculating the utilization.  And, landlord can plan how to use the resources better, not rely on automatic calculating.
		3. when the schedulable resource is over used, the containers will be killed to make sure obeying the landlord.


	* Yarn-1011:
		It's more like a Yarn scheduler enhancement for containers, but don't consider the external utilization at all.
		It's not proper for the scenario that running yarn on nodes with other services.
	
	* Yarn-5202:
		It's about resource overcommit, not the same purpose.

So, they look similar, but are different.  Users can use them based on their requirements.
Sunil, I've replied to Jason.  Could you please help check it too?:) Thank you!

[~yuuuuuuuu] thanks for the clarifications.

I understand there are slight nuances for your use case but generally it's good to consolidate related efforts as otherwise we will have an explosion of specialized features in YARN (already see a trend). IIUC, this is what [~jlowe] and [~sunilg] are both alluding to.

We do have the ability to reserve cluster resources as part of YARN-1051/YARN-2572/YARN-2573. Would that be sufficient or do you need to be able to do it at node level? If the latter is the case, you can consider achieving it in combination of reservation system with node labels (YARN-4193), intuition is each node that can be leased has a label (which can be it's name).

Another option is to have LeaseManager run outside of YARN _initially_ and use the dynamic node resource configuration (YARN-291) to affect the change you want.

Thoughts?

Thanks [~subru], 
Even i had the same in my mind either we can achieve the same by using the {{dynamic node resource configuration (YARN-291)}} or having a policy for the partitions to specify when to share the partition resources to others which i was trying to capture as part of YARN-4425,  
But for other suggestion
bq. combination of reservation system with node labels (YARN-4193), intuition is each node that can be leased has a label (which can be it's name)
IIUC they ([~yuuuuuuuu] ) are not having all the services running on top of YARN hence they want to share the nodes in off peak hours when these services will not be impacted. So i believe better solution would be to either externally control through Dynamic resource configuration or have policy on partitions to schedule during offpeak hours in NonExclusive mode (and ensure that the this partition is not configured to any queue.
